Every punishment should be carried out with seriousness, so that those who witness it will feel fear and be warned not to offend in the same way. Although criminals must be shamed as well as suffer pain, for their warning and disgrace, care should be taken that they do not appear completely despicable. Happy are those who are disciplined by the Lord to humble them, so that they will not be condemned with the world to destruction.

This is a lesson for farmers. It teaches us to value the animals that serve us. But we must learn not only to be just, but kind to all who are employed for the good of our souls, which are our better part.
The custom here regulated seemed to have been in Jewish law to keep inheritances distinct; now it is unlawful.

Dishonest gain always brings a curse on people's property, families, and souls. Happy are those who judge themselves, repent of and forsake their sins, and put away evil things, so that they may not be condemned by the Lord.

Let every persecutor and injurer of God’s people take warning from the case of the Amalekites. The longer it is before judgment comes, the more dreadful it will be at last. Amalek may remind us of the enemies of our souls. May we be enabled to destroy all our lusts, all corruptions both within and without, all the powers of darkness and of the world, which oppose our way to the blessed Savior.
